title: Saint Giong Defeating the An Invaders
summary: The legend of the boy from Giong village who did not speak or smile, but when invaders threatened, he grew into a giant hero, riding an iron horse to crush the enemy.

slides.text: During the reign of the sixth Hung King, a young child suddenly spoke when the king's messengers were searching for talented people to fight invaders.
slides.imagePrompt: A little boy Giong sitting silently, then suddenly speaking to the royal messenger, amazed parents, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Giong had an extraordinary appetite and grew as fast as the wind. The entire village contributed rice to feed him so he could fight for the country.
slides.imagePrompt: Young Giong growing up rapidly, eating big bowls of rice, villagers bringing food, happy and caring community, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: When the enemy arrived, Giong suddenly transformed into a giant hero, wearing iron armor, wielding an iron whip, and riding a fire-breathing iron horse.
slides.imagePrompt: Giong transforming into a giant hero, wearing iron armor, holding an iron whip, riding a flaming iron horse, epic transformation, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Hero Giong charged into the enemy lines. When his iron whip broke, he uprooted bamboo groves along the road to continue the fight.
slides.imagePrompt: Hero Giong using a bamboo bush as a weapon to fight enemies, iron horse breathing fire, brave and powerful, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The invaders fled in terror. Giong pursued them to the foot of Soc Mountain, took off his armor, and flew to heaven with his horse.
slides.imagePrompt: Giong on his horse flying to the sky from Soc Son mountain, leaving armor behind, heroic departure, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: During which Hung King's reign did Saint Giong fight the invaders?
When his iron whip broke, what did Saint Giong use as a weapon?
